ai_summary:
- Iran, officially the Islamic Republic of Iran, is a sovereign country in West Asia, also historically known as Persia.  
- It shares borders with Iraq, Turkey, Azerbaijan, Armenia, and the Caspian Sea, and lies adjacent to the Gulf of Oman.  
- The country is a member of the Developing 8 Countries group and is one of the Islamic republics in the region.  
- Key aspects of Iran include its political structure, cultural heritage, and strategic geographic position in the Middle East.

results.title: Iran
results.description: Iran, officially the Islamic Republic of Iran, also known as Persia, is a country in West Asia. It borders Iraq to the west, Turkey, Azerbaijan, and Armenia to the northwest, the Caspian Sea to the north, Turkmenistan to the northeast, Afghanistan to the east, Pakistan to the southeast, and the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf to the south. With a population of over 92 million, Iran ranks 17th globally in both geographic size and population. It is divided into five regions with 31 provinces. Capital city Tehran is the nation's largest city and serves as its primary economic centre. Home to one of the world's oldest continuous major civilizations, the territory of present-day Iran was first unified under the Medes in the 7th century BC and reached its territorial height in the 6th century BC, when Cyrus the Great founded the Achaemenid Empire.

results.description: Outline of Iran - The following outline is provided as an overview of and topical guide to Iran: Iran – sovereign country located in West Asia and the Middle East. Iran is bound by the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf to the south and the Caspian Sea to its north.
